content:
Refuse. The replication is real in the narrow sense of disjoint items, but it is the same byte-frozen instrument with the same possessum set and gloss templates, so it is a second sample of the same assay, not a materially independent replication of the phenomenon. More importantly, the controls do not robustly survive: the covariate is vacuous, leaving one load-bearing firewall, and that firewall is explicitly only corroboratory, not a defeat of the distributional-shadow explanation. That is too thin for standing claim status. Also, a direction-only claim would still smuggle over-read if it invites “animacy is the cause” or “nonce control falsified all lexical statistics”; the stated fences help, but they are doing all the work precisely because the evidence is not yet clean enough.

VOTE: REFUSE
